what_it_does: Defines the Registry canvas, re-framed as a 'Workshop Studio Floor' — instead of a typed-database browser it presents the type registry (window.CV_REGISTRY) organized by what the user can make (decks, brochures, widgets, wizards, shared materials). Each workstation has shelves that query the registry by layer/family and render TypeThumb tiles; users can search, inspect a type, add new types via a QuickAdder, and open an advanced Type Builder.
observations: WORKSTATIONS is a hardcoded spine of five groups, each with a headline/sub/addPrompt and shelves whose query objects ({layer, family}) drive R.query.
Reads/queries window.CV_REGISTRY and subscribes to it (R.subscribe) bumping a tick to re-render on registry changes — UI is a projection of the registry.
userAdditions counts types whose provenance is 'user' or 'vi' per workstation, shown as a '+N custom' badge.

Composes sub-components: RegistryFloor, Workstation, Shelf, ShelfTile, FloatingAddButton (defined here) plus TypeInspector, ViStudioRail, QuickAdder, TypeThumb (referenced as window.* or assumed in scope).
Shelf supports an onlyExtending filter (e.g. 'doc.wizard') applied after query, and threads the search string into the query.
ShelfTile is thumbnail-first with name/provenance revealed on hover; tile class encodes provenance (cv-prov-<provenance>).
QuickAdder onSaved opens the new type in the inspector; onAdvanced routes to the builder.
Registers window.Registry; aliases hooks as *_reg (useRef_reg imported but unused at top level).
standouts: The deliberate reframing (comment block: 'instead of a typed database browser, the Registry is a walk through your Workshop's capabilities... organised by WHAT YOU CAN MAKE, not by abstract layer').
The interface is constructed as a pure projection of CV_REGISTRY via query + subscribe, matching the project's 'interface is a projection of the registries' non-negotiable.
Provenance-aware presentation (built-in vs user vs vi) surfaced both as per-tile tags and per-workstation custom counts.
Vi is always present via ViStudioRail, contextualized to the inspected type.
conventions: Follows window-global registration and React-via-globals; reads CV_REGISTRY through register/query/subscribe as the project mandates.
Uses rs-* class names (a distinct namespace from dsa-*) for this workshop-floor layout.
Extend-by-registration philosophy mirrored in the UI: 'Add' shelves and a QuickAdder register new types rather than hand-laying components.
concerns: Two components (Registry and Shelf) each independently call R.subscribe in an effect with [] deps; the returned unsubscribe is returned from the effect (ok) but the double-subscription pattern means many subscribers — minor, but worth noting.
useRef_reg and the shelf-level effect's subscribe duplicate the parent's subscription; potential redundant re-renders.
Several referenced components (TypeInspector, ViStudioRail, QuickAdder) are not defined in this file, so the file alone cannot confirm their contracts.
WORKSTATIONS queries hardcode layer/family taxonomy here; if the registry's families change this spine must be hand-updated.
notes: The registry browser reimagined as a capability-oriented 'workshop floor': workstations→shelves→thumbnail tiles projected live from CV_REGISTRY, with inspect/add/advanced-build and an ever-present Vi rail.
questions: Where are TypeInspector, ViStudioRail, and QuickAdder defined and what are their full prop contracts?
What does onOpenBuilder open (the 'Advanced' Type Builder) and where does it live?
Is the WORKSTATIONS family taxonomy authoritative, or derived from the registry elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the file's own header comment states the intent (capability-oriented workshop floor, edit-on-thumb, Vi always present) and the implementation delivers that structure.
apparent_themes: Interface as a live projection of the registry
Organize by what-you-can-make, not abstract layer
Provenance transparency (built-in vs user vs vi) and extend-by-registration
intention_signals: Aims to make the type registry approachable by framing it around the user's real questions ('what goes inside your decks?') and keeping the UI a synchronized projection of CV_REGISTRY, with Vi and a quick-add path always at hand.
novelty: Reframes a registry/database browser as a 'workshop floor' of capability questions, with thumbnail-first tiles and provenance-driven styling, rather than a typed-layer table.
contribution: Contributes the capability-oriented Registry canvas: a workstation→shelf→tile projection of CV_REGISTRY (queried by layer/family, filtered by search/extends), provenance badges/counts, and the inspect/add/advanced-build + Vi-rail composition.
summary_for_embedding: A React Registry 'Workshop Floor' canvas that projects window.CV_REGISTRY as workstations and shelves of thumbnail type-tiles (queried by layer/family), with search, provenance counts, inspect, quick-add, and an always-present Vi rail.
intention_for_embedding: To make the type registry navigable by what the user can make rather than abstract layers, as a live projection of CV_REGISTRY with inspect/add/Vi assistance built in.
